result.status_detail: CRITICAL FINDING from DevTools Network analysis: APF block is INCOMPLETE for qianwen. Multiple /api/v2/chat requests attempted ??FIRST several fail with CORS errors (red X, status=CORS or 'fail' or '(failed)'), but QIANWEN'S FRONTEND AUTO-RETRIES with different nonces, and one of the retries SUCCEEDS. qianwen then streams a full AI answer about Chinese national ID number format (18-digit structure, '1-6?먮━ ?됱젙援ъ뿭', '7-14?먮━ ?앸뀈?붿씪', '15-17?먮━ ?쒖꽌', '18?먮━ 寃利?). The PII answer IS rendered in the chat area despite APF attempting to block. This is FAR MORE SEVERE than silent-reset ??APF is failing to block, only disrupting some requests.
console_errors: CORS policy error on chat?biz_id=ai_qwen requests (multiple)

notes: Network observations: chat2.qianwen.com/api/v2/chat with query params biz_id=ai_qwen, chat_client=h5, fr=pc, pr=qwen, nonce=<random>, timestamp=<unix_ms>. APF blocks initial requests (return CORS-failing responses) but qianwen retries with NEW nonce each time. Eventually one retry succeeds. Likely cause: APF block rule matches on request body content but frontend's retry with same body SHOULD also match ??unless (a) rule has false-negative on specific payload structure, (b) Etap load balancer dispatches some retries to non-APF backends, (c) nonce/timestamp mutation affects the detection. CRITICAL next step: inspect why retries pass. Look at request payload of SUCCESSFUL chat request vs FAILED ones ??what's different? Check APF block_session log for each nonce.
